titles.text: AN OPEN-LABEL, MULTICENTER, NON-RANDOMIZED PHASE 2 STUDY OF ELRANATAMAB (PF-06863135) MONOTHERAPY IN PARTICIPANTS WITH MULTIPLE MYELOMA WHO ARE REFRACTORY TO AT LEAST ONE PROTEASOME INHIBITOR, ONE IMMUNOMODULATORY DRUG AND ONE ANTI-CD38 ANTIBODY
A Phase 2 Study of Elranatamab (PF-06863135) Monotherapy in Participants With MM Who Are Refractory to at Least One PI, One IMiD and One Anti-CD38 mAb

abbreviations.expandedText: National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events
American Society for Transplantation and Cellular Therapy

amendments.name: Amendment 1

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: 1. Throughout: Removed inclusion of participants age <18 years and all corresponding assent language (including removing reference to “legally authorized representative” in Section 10.1.3). 2. SoA, Section 8.1.1 and Appendix 10: FLC now specified as “serum FLC” for clarity. 3. SoA, Section 1.1, Section 4.3, Section 6.1.1 and Section 6.6.3: Removed investigator’s discretion with regard to the transitions between dosing intervals (QW, Q2W). 4. SoA and Section 8.2.4: Clarified timing of ECGs: removed “postdose” as there are no scheduled postdose collections, and removed the 60-minute predose window. 5. SoA: Added rows for “Disease response assessment (per IMWG criteria)” and “Assessment of BOR” 6. SoA: Added detail around timing of UPEP, UIFE and imaging. Additional detail added to align optional BMB sampling with optional BMA sampling. 7. SoA and Section 8.2.7: Added “serum test required at screening” for clarity in alignment with footnote in Table 8 of Appendix 2. 8. SoA: Removed “within 2 hour” requirement for biomarker sample collection. 9. SoA: Added sBCMA samples beyond Cycle 7 to characterize the impact of Q2W switch on sBCMA levels from Cycle 7 and on. 10. Section 4.2.1: Modified to remove mention of participants in C1071001 who have previously received BCMA-directed BsAb. 11. Sections 4.2.2: Added MRD threshold of 10-5, with the potential to measure MRD frequency up to 10-6. 12. Section 4.4: Modified to specify that the study will be completed when all participants have been followed for OS for at least 2 years from the date of enrollment. 13. Section 5.1 (Inclusion criterion #15): Removed “except for AEs not constituting a safety risk for the participant by investigator judgment” 14. Section 5.2 (Exclusion criteria #2, #3 and #6): Modified for clarity. 15. Section 6.1: Corrected vial size from 2 mL to 6 mL. 16. Section 6.1.1: Corrected timing of PF-06863135 administration from Day 21 to Day 22. 17. Section 6.6.1: Updated redosing criteria for clarity. 18. Section 6.6.1 and Table 4: Removed “or is Grade 2 and is not considered by the investigator to be a safety risk to the participant” 19. Table 4 footnote b: Added “or permanent discontinuation.” 20. Section 8.1: Removed “assessment of best response, date of response onset, and date of progression (if applicable)” 21. Section 8.1.1: Minor edits for clarity 22. Section 8.1.5: Added “(as applicable)” to “Best response and date of disease progression…” 23. Section 8.2.2: Removed “Any abnormal physical examination findings observed prior to administration of the first dose of PF-06863135 should be recorded in the Medical History CRF.” 24. Section 8.3.1: Removed “for survival” from “During the long-term follow-up period in this study…” 25. Section 8.8.1: Added MRD threshold of 10-5. 26. Section 8.8.4 – Section 8.8.7: Removed sample volumes as these will be specified in the laboratory manual. Also added “(for predose samples, collection should occur prior to administration of PF-06863135 on that day)” to clarify that predose sample collections are to occur on the same day as treatment. 27. Section 9.1.2: Added rationale for hypotheses. 28. Section 9.2: Changed power from 80% to 90% to detect ORR of 48% (previously 45%) in Cohort A and 34% (previously 31%) in Cohort B. 29. Section 9.4.5: Added section on other safety analyses. 30. Section 9.5 (with references to Section 9.5 added to Section 2.3.1 and Section 8.3.8): Added interim safety assessment section for Grade ≥3 CRS, Grade ≥3 ICANS, Grade ≥4 non-hematologic events (excluding CRS and ICANS) and Grade 5 hematologic events. Also added “If the efficacy boundary is crossed, enrollment to the study will continue up to the specified number of participants, and all ongoing participants will continue with scheduled visits per the Schedule of Activities” to clarify that the study will not be stopped for efficacy at IA. 31. Appendix 2 (Table 8): Added LDH to lab assessments. Appendix 10: Removed “Participants will continue in the last confirmed response category until there is confirmation of progression or improvement to a higher response status.”

amendments.name: Amendment 5

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: This amendment incorporates changes previously included in Amendment 2 (which was based on Protocol Amendment 1) and country-specific Amendments 3 and 4 (which were also based on Protocol Amendment 1); therefore Protocol Amendment 5 brings all the amendments into one document. Following is a summary of additional changes: 1. As per regulatory requirements (US FDA), updates to describe peripheral neuropathy (including GBS) as an important potential risk of elranatamab, and measures to mitigate risk including (a) addition of various new safety monitoring measures, (b) modification to participant selection (exclusion) for those potentially at higher risk; (c) addition of dose modification rules for peripheral neuropathy; (d) addition of recommended work-up for peripheral neuropathy; and (e) addition of considerations regarding concomitant medications. In addition, the criteria for placing the study on temporary hold (specifically related to neuropathy or other IR neurological AEs) were fully defined. 2. Additional information was added in Background (Section 2.2) based on Phase 1 results. 3. To mitigate CRS and ICANS, a 2 step-up priming dose approach (which includes premedication, and administration of elranatamab on C1D1 and C1D4 before the first full dose of elranatamab) was incorporated for the first week of study intervention. 4. An additional visit, hospitalization periods, safety assessments, sample collections, and PROs are included in the SoA and Section 6. 5. For participant selection, clarification was added around DVT and window for prior transplant. 6. In order to capture all potential AEs with elranatamab, including late onset immune-related neurologic AEs, the safety reporting period after last dose of study intervention has been increased to 90 days. 7. Contraception use has been extended from 28 days to 90 days after the last dose of study intervention (as required by France HA). 8. A Steering Committee, to be involved with general oversight of the study, was established. 9. Staging systems for multiple myeloma were more explicitly defined. 10. Interim analysis set populations (for both cohort A and B) were further defined. 11. Given the INN name is established, PF-06863135 has been replaced with the INN name, elranatamab. 12. Added Appendix 12: Japan Regulatory Requirements. 13. Clarifications were made throughout, including the SoA.

amendments.name: Amendment 6

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: 1. Revised enrollment hold rules per regulatory requirement (US FDA): the posterior probability threshold of ≥80% is used for stopping rules for safety for treatment-related Grade 3-4 GBS (including variants), and treatment-related Peripheral Neuropathy/IR Neurologic AEs (section 9.5.2 and Appendix 13). 2. Exclusion criteria for ongoing Grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy was revised (per regulatory requirements, US FDA). 3. Dose modifications for peripheral neuropathy were revised (per regulatory requirements, US FDA). 4. Clarification added for dose delays/interruptions, including windows (SoA, section 6.1.1, section 6.6.2). 5. Clarified imaging schedule, including requirement that imaging be conducted every 12 weeks for participants with baseline extramedullary disease (SoA). 6. Clarification added for availability of study treatment after end of study (section 6.7). 7. Clarified requirements for laboratory assessments (section 8.1.1). 8. Removed requirement for investigator review of completed PRO responses (including EORTC QLQ CIPN20, as regular neurologic exams are performed by the investigator) (section 8.1.4, and reference in section 2.3.1). 9. Clarified requirements for exposure during pregnancy (section 8.3.5.1). 10. Clarifications were made throughout, including SoA and Appendices.

Removed requirement for investigator review of completed PRO responses (including EORTC QLQ CIPN20, as regular neurologic exams are performed by the investigator) (section 8.1.4, and reference in section 2.3.1). 9. Clarified requirements for exposure during pregnancy (section 8.3.5.1). 10. Clarifications were made throughout, including SoA and Appendices.

amendments.name: Amendment 7

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: 1. The total number of planned participants was revised from 150 to 180 participants allowing for more robust datasets. Power/sample size calculations updated to be based on normal distribution from exact method (sections 1, 4.1, 9.2, 9.5). 2. Interim analyses (IA) revised to include a more robust data set (increased N from 60 to 90 for Cohort A) and added efficacy IA for Cohort B. IAs to be based on actual number with adequate follow-up at time of IA (section 9.5). 3. The follow-up period for final analysis was revised (section 9.2). 4. Added statement that interim analyses results can be used by the sponsor for decision-making and a statement about requirements before interim analysis can be conducted in accordance with the sponsor’s SOPs (sections 7, 9.5). 5. Clarification that permitted concomitant treatments include infection prophylaxis and use of IV immunoglobulins; added further considerations for oral contraceptives (section 6.5.2). 6. Clarification for dose modifications, including those for priming doses (sections 6.6.1, 6.6.2, Table 4). 7. Revised local laboratory collections to include serum quantitative immunoglobulins and plasma cells (SoA, Appendix 2). 8. Revised BMA sampling times; clarified BMA sampling times in cases of CR by all other parameters except bone marrow plasma cells (SoA). 9. Made BMB sampling optional to better align with global clinical practice (SoA, section 8.1.2, 8.8.3). 10. Added immunofluorescence and flow cytometry for the determination of sCR to better align with global clinical practice (section 8.1.2, Appendix 10). 11. Clarified allowed sample types for MRD evaluation (SoA, section 8.8.1). 12. Added allowance for use of sample in the event that the index myeloma clone is not identified from screening sample (section 8.8.1) 13. Reduced frequency of administration of PROs after Year 1 (SoA). 14. Clarified imaging requirements after EMD resolution/disappearance (SoA). 15. Removed BOR assessment (SoA). 16. For discontinuation reasons, added lack of efficacy and completed (sections 7.1 and 7.2). 17. Added more detailed description for MRD analysis (section 9.4.3). 18. Clarifications were made throughout multiple sections, including section 1, 3-5, 8, SoA and Appendices.

amendments.name: Amendment 10

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: 1. Revised elranatamab contraception requirements (WOCBP: aligned with updated elranatamab PK data as described in IB; Men: aligned with calculated safety margin between seminal transfer and estimated MABEL). (Sections 1.3, 4.2.3, 5.3.1, 10.4). 2. Revised elranatamab clinical study summaries, referring protocol users to the IB for the most current information (Sections 2.2.3, 2.3.1). 3. Added more detailed descriptions in Sections 1.3 and 8.1.1 for when serum FLC should be used to assess disease response and about repeat sampling for confirmation of disease progression to match descriptions in Section 10.10. 4. Added definition of ‘Final Analysis Evaluable Set’ to align with statistical analysis plan (Section 9.3). 5. Revised descriptions throughout Section 10.1 to improve clarity/provide additional detail (including, but not limited to, added description of study data collection, processing and storage systems [Section 10.1.4]; clarification of protection of personally identifiable information [Section 10.1.5]; description of QTLs [Section 10.1.6]; clarifications for study start and closure [Section 10.1.8]; clarification of publication policy [Section10.1.9]; clarifications about contact card [Section 10.1.10]). 6. Removed requirement to notify study team when treatment is restarted following SARS-CoV-2 infection as protocol already includes relevant guidance (Section 10.8.4). 7. General editorial revision throughout to specify “confirmed” PD in descriptions of study intervention discontinuation and disease response requirements for consistency with descriptions in Sections 6.1.1 and 8.1. 8. General editorial revisions throughout to correct grammatical errors, to maintain consistency, and/or to improve clarity. 9. Sponsor legal address added (page 1). This amendment incorporates all revisions to date, including amendments made at the request of country health authorities and IRBs/ECs and any protocol administrative change letter(s).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Age and Sex
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Male or female participants age ≥18 years.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Compliance with study procedures
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants who are willing and able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior diagnosis of MM
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Prior diagnosis of MM as defined according to IMWG criteria (Rajkumar et al, 2014).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Measurable disease
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Measurable disease based on IMWG criteria as defined by at least 1 of the following:
a. Serum M-protein >0.5 g/dL by SPEP
b. Urinary M-protein excretion >200 mg/24 hours by UPEP
c. Serum immunoglobulin FLC ≥10 mg/dL (≥100 mg/L) AND abnormal serum immunoglobulin kappa to lambda FLC ratio (<0.26 or >1.65)

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Refractory to IMiD
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Refractory to at least one IMiD (as defined in Note below).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Refractory to PI
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Refractory to at least one PI (as defined in Note below).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Refractory to anti-CD38 antibody
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Refractory to at least one anti-CD38 antibody (as defined in Note below).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Relapsed or refractory to last anti-MM regimen
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Relapsed or refractory to last anti-MM regimen. Note: Refractory is defined as having disease progression while on therapy or within 60 days of last dose in any line, regardless of response.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior BCMA-directed therapy status
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Cohort A: Has not received prior BCMA-directed therapy.
Cohort B: Has received prior BCMA-directed ADC or BCMA-directed CAR T-cell therapy, either approved or investigational.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: ECOG performance status
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: ECOG performance status ≤2.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: LVEF
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: LVEF ≥40% as determined by a MUGA scan or ECHO.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate hepatic function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Adequate hepatic function characterized by the following:
a. Total bilirubin ≤2 x ULN (≤3 x ULN if documented Gilbert’s syndrome);
b. AST ≤2.5 x ULN; and
c. ALT ≤2.5 x ULN

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate renal function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Adequate renal function defined by an estimated creatinine clearance ≥30 mL/min (according to the Cockcroft Gault formula, by 24-hour urine collection for creatinine clearance, or according to local institutional standard method).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate BM function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Adequate BM function characterized by the following:
a. ANC ≥1.0 × 109/L (use of granulocyte-colony stimulating factors is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing);
b. Platelets ≥25 × 109/L (transfusion support is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing); and
c. Hemoglobin ≥8 g/dL (transfusion support is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Resolved acute effects of prior therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Resolved acute effects of any prior therapy to baseline severity or CTCAE Grade ≤1.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Informed consent
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix 1, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICD and in this protocol.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Smoldering MM
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Smoldering MM.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Active plasma cell leukemia
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Active plasma cell leukemia.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Amyloidosis
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Amyloidosis.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: POEMS syndrome
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: POEMS syndrome

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Stem cell transplant or active GVHD
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Stem cell transplant within 12 weeks prior to enrollment or active GVHD.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Impaired cardiovascular function
Ongoing peripheral neuropathy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Ongoing Grade ≥2 peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: History of peripheral neuropathy with prior BCMA-directed therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: History of any grade peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy with prior BCMA-directed therapy (Cohort B).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: History of GBS or severe peripheral motor polyneuropathy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: History of GBS or GBS variants, or history of any Grade ≥3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Active infections
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Active HBV, HCV, SARS-CoV2, HIV, or any active, uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection. Active infections must be resolved at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Other active malignancy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Any other active malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment, except for adequately treated basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer, or carcinoma in situ.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Other significant medical/psychiatric conditions or surgery
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Other surgical (including major surgery within 14 days prior to enrollment), medical or psychiatric conditions including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator’s judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Previous anti-BCMA bispecific antibody treatment
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Previous treatment with an anti-BCMA bispecific antibody.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Previous investigational drug administration
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Previous administration with an investigational drug within 30 days (or as determined by the local requirement) or 5 half-lives preceding the first dose of study intervention used in this study (whichever is longer).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Study staff or family members
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Hypersensitivity to study intervention
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Known or suspected hypersensitivity to the study intervention or any of its excipients.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Live attenuated vaccine administration
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Live attenuated vaccine must not be administered within 4 weeks of the first dose of study intervention.

arms.name: Elranatamab Monotherapy
arms.description: All participants receive Elranatamab via subcutaneous administration with a step-up priming regimen (12 mg on Cycle 1 Day 1, 32 mg on Cycle 1 Day 4) followed by a maintenance dose of 76 mg on Cycle 1 Day 8 and thereafter. The frequency of the 76 mg dose changes from weekly (QW) for at least 6 cycles to every two weeks (Q2W) from Cycle 7 Day 1 if response criteria are met.

estimands.name: Primary Efficacy Estimand: Overall Response Rate
estimands.populationSummary: Adult patients with Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM) refractory to at least one Proteasome Inhibitor (PI), one Immunomodulatory Drug (IMiD), and one anti-CD38 monoclonal antibody (mAb). This includes Cohort A (BCMA-naïve) and Cohort B (previously exposed to BCMA-directed therapy). (Participants who received at least one dose of study intervention (Cohort A and Cohort B)) Summary measure: Proportion of participants with an Overall Response (OR) and 2-sided 95% Confidence Interval.

intercurrentEvents.name: Subsequent anticancer therapy (Primary Efficacy Estimand: Overall Response Rate)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participant receives anticancer therapies other than the study intervention prior to achieving an Overall Response (OR).

intercurrentEvents.name: Confirmed disease progression (PD) (Primary Efficacy Estimand: Overall Response Rate)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participant experiences confirmed disease progression prior to achieving an Overall Response (OR).

intercurrentEvents.name: Death (Primary Efficacy Estimand: Overall Response Rate)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participant dies prior to achieving an Overall Response (OR).

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation of disease assessments (Primary Efficacy Estimand: Overall Response Rate)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participant stops disease assessments for any reason (e.g., study intervention discontinuation, withdrawal of consent) prior to achieving an Overall Response (OR).

intercurrentEvents.name: Lack of post-baseline disease assessment (Primary Efficacy Estimand: Overall Response Rate)
intercurrentEvents.text: Participant does not have a post-baseline disease assessment due to early confirmed disease progression (PD).
